definition_population.content: Primary ITP is an acquired autoimmune disorder with antibody-mediated platelet destruction and impaired production, no identifiable secondary cause; affects children (often self-limited) and adults (often chronic).

epidemiology.content: ITP incidence: 1.6-3.9 per 100,000 patient-years, increasing with age, slight female preponderance. Standard duration classification: acute <3mo, persistent 3-12mo, chronic >12mo.

sources.quote: Immune thrombocytopenia (ITP) occurs with an incidence rate of 1.6 to 3.9 per 100,000 patient-years, which increases with age and has a slight female preponderance
ITP is termed acute, persistent or chronic when its duration is <3 months, 3 to 12 months and >12 months, respectively

biology.content: TMEM131 has no direct PubMed literature link to thrombocytopenia — not established in this pass. ITP pathophysiology involves autoantibody-mediated platelet clearance and platelet apoptosis; general CD4+ T-cell help for autoreactive B cells is the field-standard autoimmune mechanism, but no direct TMEM131 tie was found.

sources.quote: Given the low platelet counts in patients with immune thrombocytopenia (< 100 ×10/L) and its pathophysiological complexity, it remains both a diagnostic and therapeutic challenge

treatment.content: Thrombopoietin receptor agonists (TPO-RAs) including eltrombopag (FDA-approved, 11 Drugs@FDA records) are established second-line ITP therapy; newer oral agents like hetrombopag are being studied in pediatric refractory ITP.

sources.quote: thrombopoietin receptor agonists (TPO-RAs) are effective second-line treatments for pediatric immune thrombocytopenia (ITP), real-world data on newer agents like hetrombopag remain scarce

regulatory_trials.content: ClinicalTrials.gov: 493 total interventional trials under 'immune thrombocytopenia'; 171 total trials under the literal label 'primary thrombocytopenia'.

sources.source_url: (no URL returned by tool; MCP clinical-trials search_trials result, condition="immune thrombocytopenia", total=493)
sources.quote: total=493 (search_trials API, condition='immune thrombocytopenia', study_type=INTERVENTIONAL, count_total=True)

gaps: Disease label ambiguity: 'primary thrombocytopenia' could denote ITP (interpreted here, matching current 'primary immune thrombocytopenia' nomenclature) or be confused with essential thrombocythemia (elevated platelets, a distinct MPN) — flagged explicitly.
TMEM131-ITP mechanistic link not established in this pass despite OT genetic-association score of 0.39.
